webcam(3) Library Functions Manual webcam(3)

NAME

webcam - Webcam Module


- 📷 Cross-platform webcam capture API

SYNOPSIS

Files


file webcam_v4l2.c
📷 Linux V4L2 webcam capture with multi-format support file webcam_avfoundation.m
📷 macOS AVFoundation webcam capture implementation with hardware acceleration file webcam_wasm.c
WebAssembly (browser) webcam capture backend. file webcam_mediafoundation.c
📷 Windows Media Foundation webcam capture with hardware acceleration support

Data Structures


struct webcam_device_info_t
Webcam device information structure.

Macros


#define WEBCAM_DEVICE_NAME_MAX 256
Maximum length of webcam device name.

Typedefs


typedef struct webcam_context_t webcam_context_t
Opaque webcam context structure.

Functions


asciichat_error_t webcam_list_devices (webcam_device_info_t **devices, unsigned int *count)
Enumerate available webcam devices. void webcam_free_device_list (webcam_device_info_t *devices)
Free device list returned by webcam_list_devices(). asciichat_error_t webcam_init (unsigned short int webcam_index)
Initialize global webcam interface. image_t * webcam_read (void)
Capture a frame from global webcam. void webcam_destroy (void)
Clean up global webcam interface. void webcam_flush (void)
Flush/interrupt any pending webcam read operations. void webcam_print_init_error_help (asciichat_error_t error_code)
Print helpful error diagnostics for webcam initialization failures. asciichat_error_t webcam_init_context (webcam_context_t **ctx, unsigned short int device_index)
Initialize webcam context for advanced operations. void webcam_cleanup_context (webcam_context_t *ctx)
Clean up webcam context and release resources. void webcam_flush_context (webcam_context_t *ctx)
Flush/interrupt pending read operations on webcam context. image_t * webcam_read_context (webcam_context_t *ctx)
Capture a frame from webcam context. image_t * webcam_read_async (webcam_context_t *ctx)
Get the most recent frame from async camera thread (non-blocking). asciichat_error_t webcam_get_dimensions (webcam_context_t *ctx, int *width, int *height)
Get webcam frame dimensions.

Detailed Description

📷 Cross-platform webcam capture API

This header provides a cross-platform webcam capture interface for ascii-chat. The system abstracts platform-specific webcam APIs (Windows Media Foundation, Linux V4L2, macOS AVFoundation) behind a unified interface for video frame capture.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Cross-platform webcam access (Windows, Linux, macOS)
  • Device enumeration and selection
  • Real-time video frame capture
  • Automatic format conversion to RGB
  • Thread-safe context management
  • Error handling with helpful diagnostics

PLATFORM SUPPORT:

  • Windows: Media Foundation API
  • Linux: Video4Linux2 (V4L2)
  • macOS: AVFoundation framework

ARCHITECTURE:

The webcam system uses a context-based architecture:

  • Global context for simple single-webcam scenarios
  • Per-context management for multi-webcam support
  • Automatic format detection and conversion
  • Frame rate management and throttling

VIDEO FORMATS:

The system supports:

  • Various native formats (YUY2, RGB, MJPEG, etc.)
  • Automatic conversion to RGB for processing
  • Format negotiation with webcam hardware
  • Frame rate configuration

Note

The global webcam interface (webcam_init, webcam_read) provides simple single-webcam access. Use context-based functions for advanced multi-webcam scenarios.

Webcam frames are returned as image_t structures compatible with the video conversion pipeline.

Error codes include specific diagnostics for webcam issues (permission denied, device in use, etc.).

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

August 2025

Macro Definition Documentation

#define WEBCAM_DEVICE_NAME_MAX 256

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>

Maximum length of webcam device name.

Definition at line 77 of file webcam.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct webcam_context_t webcam_context_t

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>

Opaque webcam context structure. Forward declaration of webcam context for context-based operations. Context provides per-webcam state management for advanced scenarios.

Note

Use webcam_init_context() to create a new context.

Context must be cleaned up with webcam_cleanup_context().

Definition at line 153 of file webcam.h.

Function Documentation

void webcam_cleanup_context (webcam_context_t * ctx)

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>

Clean up webcam context and release resources.

Parameters

ctx Webcam context to clean up (can be NULL)

Cleans up a webcam context and releases all associated resources. Closes the webcam device, frees memory, and invalidates the context.

Note

Safe to call multiple times or with NULL pointer (no-op).

After cleanup, context pointer is invalid and must not be used.

Definition at line 253 of file video/webcam/webcam.c.

253                                                    {
254   (void)ctx;
255   log_warn("Webcam cleanup called on unsupported platform");
256 }




References log_warn.


Referenced by media_source_destroy(), and
    webcam_destroy().






void
  webcam_destroy (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>


Clean up global webcam interface. Cleans up the global webcam
    interface and releases resources. Closes the webcam device and frees all
    associated memory.




Note


Safe to call multiple times (no-op after first call).



After cleanup, webcam_read() will fail until
    webcam_init() is called again.




Definition at line 137 of file
    video/webcam/webcam.c.


137                           {
138   // Free cached webcam frame if it was allocated (from test pattern or real webcam)
139   if (cached_webcam_frame) {
140     image_destroy(cached_webcam_frame);
141     cached_webcam_frame = NULL;
142   }
143 
144   if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
145     log_debug("Test pattern mode - webcam context cleanup skipped");
146     return;
147   }
148 
149   if (global_webcam_ctx) {
150     NAMED_UNREGISTER(global_webcam_ctx);
151     webcam_cleanup_context(global_webcam_ctx);
152     global_webcam_ctx = NULL;
153     // log_info("Webcam resources released");
154   } else {
155     log_dev("Webcam was not opened, nothing to release");
156   }
157 }






References GET_OPTION, image_destroy(),
    log_debug, log_dev, NAMED_UNREGISTER, and
    webcam_cleanup_context().


Referenced by ascii_read_destroy().






void
  webcam_flush (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>


Flush/interrupt any pending webcam read operations. Cancels any
    blocking ReadSample operations. Call this before stopping the capture thread
    to allow it to exit cleanly.


Definition at line 159 of file
    video/webcam/webcam.c.


159                         {
160   if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
161     return; // Test pattern doesn't need flushing
162   }
163 
164   if (global_webcam_ctx) {
165     webcam_flush_context(global_webcam_ctx);
166   }
167 }






References GET_OPTION, and
  webcam_flush_context().






void
  webcam_flush_context (webcam_context_t * ctx)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>


Flush/interrupt pending read operations on webcam context.


Parameters


ctx Webcam context (may be NULL - no-op)


Cancels any blocking read operations. Call before stopping capture
    thread.

    video/webcam/webcam.c.

devices Device list to free (can be NULL)

asciichat_error_t webcam_get_dimensions
  (webcam_context_t * ctx, int * width, int * height)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>


Get webcam frame dimensions.


Parameters


ctx Webcam context (must not be NULL)


width Output pointer for frame width (must not be NULL)


height Output pointer for frame height (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Queries the webcam context for current frame dimensions. Returns
    the width and height in pixels as determined during format negotiation with
    the webcam hardware.




Note


Dimensions may change if webcam format is renegotiated.



Frame dimensions are set during webcam_init_context().




Definition at line 269 of file
    video/webcam/webcam.c.


269                                                                                         {
270   (void)ctx;
271   (void)width;
272   (void)height;
273   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_WEBCAM, "Webcam get dimensions not supported on this platform");
274 }






References ERROR_WEBCAM, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by webcam_init().






asciichat_error_t webcam_init (unsigned short int
  webcam_index)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>


Initialize global webcam interface.


Parameters


webcam_index Webcam device index (0 for default
  device)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Initializes the global webcam interface for simple single-webcam
    access. Opens the specified webcam device and prepares it for frame capture.
    This is a convenience wrapper around webcam_init_context() for simple
    single-webcam scenarios.




Note


This function initializes global webcam state.



Use webcam_read() to capture frames after
  initialization.




Must call webcam_destroy() when done.




Use webcam_init_context() for advanced multi-webcam
    scenarios.




Warning


On failure, use webcam_print_init_error_help() for
  diagnostics.


Definition at line 21 of file
  video/webcam/webcam.c.


21                                                                {
22   // Check if test pattern mode is enabled
23   if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
24     log_info("Test pattern mode enabled - not opening real webcam");
25     log_info("Test pattern resolution: 320x240");
26     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
27   }
28 
29 #ifdef __linux__
30   log_info("Initializing webcam with V4L2 (Linux)");
31   log_info("Attempting to open webcam with index %d using V4L2 (Linux)...", webcam_index);
32 #elif defined(__APPLE__)
33   log_info("Initializing webcam with AVFoundation (macOS)");
34   log_info("Attempting to open webcam with index %d using AVFoundation (macOS)...", webcam_index);
35 #elif defined(_WIN32)
36   log_info("Initializing webcam with Media Foundation (Windows)");
37   log_info("Attempting to open webcam with index %d using Media Foundation (Windows)...", webcam_index);
38 #else
39   log_info("Initializing webcam with Unknown platform");
40   log_info("Attempting to open webcam with index %d using Unknown platform...", webcam_index);
41 #endif
42 
43   asciichat_error_t result = webcam_init_context(&global_webcam_ctx, webcam_index);
44   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
45     SET_ERRNO(result, "Failed to connect to webcam (error code: %d)", result);
46     return result;
47   }
48 
49   /* Register global webcam context with named registry */
50   char webcam_name[32];
51   SAFE_SNPRINTF(webcam_name, sizeof(webcam_name), "webcam:%u", webcam_index);
52   NAMED_REGISTER(global_webcam_ctx, webcam_name, "webcam", "0x%tx", NULL);
53 
54   // Get image dimensions
55   int width, height;
56   if (webcam_get_dimensions(global_webcam_ctx, &width, &height) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
57     log_info("Webcam opened successfully! Resolution: %dx%d", width, height);
58   } else {
59     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_WEBCAM, "Webcam opened but failed to get dimensions");
60   }
61 
62   return result;
63 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_WEBCAM,
    GET_OPTION, log_info, NAMED_REGISTER,
    SAFE_SNPRINTF, SET_ERRNO, webcam_get_dimensions(), and
    webcam_init_context().


Referenced by ascii_read_init().






asciichat_error_t webcam_init_context
  (webcam_context_t ** ctx, unsigned short int device_index)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>


Initialize webcam context for advanced operations.


Parameters


ctx Output pointer to webcam context (must not be
  NULL)


device_index Webcam device index (0 for default device)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Initializes a new webcam context for context-based webcam
    management. This allows multiple webcams to be used simultaneously or
    provides more control over webcam lifecycle. Context must be cleaned up with
    webcam_cleanup_context() when done.




Note


Context is allocated by this function and must be freed
  with webcam_cleanup_context().



Use webcam_read_context() to capture frames from this
    context.




Use webcam_get_dimensions() to query frame dimensions.




Warning


On failure, use webcam_print_init_error_help() for
  diagnostics.


Definition at line 246 of file
    video/webcam/webcam.c.


246                                                                                                {
247   (void)ctx;
248   (void)device_index;
249   SET_ERRNO(ERROR_WEBCAM, "Webcam platform not supported on this system");
250   return ERROR_WEBCAM;
251 }






References ERROR_WEBCAM, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by media_source_create(), and
    webcam_init().






asciichat_error_t webcam_list_devices
  (webcam_device_info_t ** devices, unsigned int * count)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>


Enumerate available webcam devices.


Parameters


devices Output pointer to array of device info
  structures (must not be NULL)


count Output pointer to number of devices found (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Enumerates all available webcam devices and returns their
    information. The devices array is allocated by this function and must be
    freed with webcam_free_device_list().




Note


On success, *devices will point to a dynamically
  allocated array.



If no devices are found, *count will be 0 and *devices will be
    NULL.




Call webcam_free_device_list() to free the returned
  array.




Example:




webcam_device_info_t *devices = NULL;
unsigned int count = 0;
if (webcam_list_devices(&devices, &count) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {


    for (unsigned int i = 0; i < count; i++) {


        printf("Device %u: %s\n", devices[i].index, devices[i].name);


    }


    webcam_free_device_list(devices);
}



Definition at line 276 of file
    video/webcam/webcam.c.


276                                                                                                    {
277   if (out_devices)
278     *out_devices = NULL;
279   if (out_count)
280     *out_count = 0;
281   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_WEBCAM, "Webcam device enumeration not supported on this platform");
282 }






References ERROR_WEBCAM, and SET_ERRNO.






void
  webcam_print_init_error_help (asciichat_error_t error_code)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>


Print helpful error diagnostics for webcam initialization
    failures.


Parameters


error_code Error code from
  webcam_init() or webcam_init_context()


Prints human-readable error diagnostics to help diagnose webcam
    initialization failures. Includes platform-specific troubleshooting advice
    for common issues (permission denied, device in use, etc.).




Note


This function prints to stderr with detailed diagnostics.



Useful for debugging webcam access issues.




Definition at line 169 of file
    video/webcam/webcam.c.


169                                                                 {
170   // Platform-specific error messages and troubleshooting help
171 #ifdef __linux__
172   safe_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
173 
174   if (error_code == ERROR_WEBCAM) {
175     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Webcam initialization failed on Linux.\n\n");
176     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Common solutions:\n");
177     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  1. Check if a camera is connected:\n");
178     safe_fprintf(stderr, "       ls /dev/video*\n\n");
179     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  2. If no camera is available, use test pattern mode:\n");
180     safe_fprintf(stderr, "       ascii-chat client --test-pattern\n\n");
181     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  3. Install V4L2 drivers if needed:\n");
182     safe_fprintf(stderr, "       sudo apt-get install v4l-utils\n");
183   } else if (error_code == ERROR_WEBCAM_PERMISSION) {
184     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Camera permission denied.\n\n");
185     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Fix permissions with:\n");
186     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  sudo usermod -a -G video $USER\n");
187     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Then log out and log back in for changes to take effect.\n");
188   } else if (error_code == ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE) {
189     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Camera is already in use by another application.\n\n");
190     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Try closing other camera apps or use test pattern mode:\n");
191     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  ascii-chat client --test-pattern\n");
192   } else {
193     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Webcam error on Linux.\n\n");
194     safe_fprintf(stderr, "General troubleshooting:\n");
195     safe_fprintf(stderr, "* Check camera: ls /dev/video*\n");
196     safe_fprintf(stderr, "* Check permissions: groups | grep video\n");
197     safe_fprintf(stderr, "* Use test pattern: ascii-chat client --test-pattern\n");
198   }
199   (void)fflush(stderr);
200 #elif defined(__APPLE__)
201   (void)error_code;
202   safe_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
203   safe_fprintf(stderr, "On macOS, you may need to grant camera permissions:\n");
204   safe_fprintf(stderr,
205                "* Say \"yes\" to the popup about system camera access that you see when running this program for the "
206                "first time.\n");
207   safe_fprintf(
208       stderr, "* If you said \"no\" to the popup, go to System Preferences > Security & Privacy > Privacy > Camera.\n");
209   safe_fprintf(stderr,
210                "   Now flip the switch next to your terminal application in that privacy list to allow ascii-chat to "
211                "access your camera.\n");
212   safe_fprintf(stderr, "   Then just run this program again.\n");
213   (void)fflush(stderr);
214 #elif defined(_WIN32)
215   if (error_code == ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE) {
216     // Device is in use by another application - this is a fatal error on Windows
217     safe_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
218     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Webcam is already in use by another application.\n");
219     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Windows allows only one application to access the webcam at a time.\n");
220     safe_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
221     safe_fprintf(stderr, "To use ascii-chat with multiple clients, try these alternatives:\n");
222     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  --test-pattern    Generate a colorful test pattern instead of using webcam\n");
223     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  --file VIDEO.mp4  Use a video file as input (to be implemented)\n");
224     safe_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
225     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Example: ascii-chat client --test-pattern\n");
226     (void)fflush(stderr);
227   } else {
228     // Other webcam errors - general failure
229     safe_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
230     safe_fprintf(stderr, "On Windows, this might be because:\n");
231     safe_fprintf(stderr, "* Camera permissions are not granted\n");
232     safe_fprintf(stderr, "* Camera driver issues\n");
233     safe_fprintf(stderr, "* No webcam device found\n");
234     (void)fflush(stderr);
235   }
236 #else
237   // Unknown platform
238   (void)error_code;
239   safe_fprintf(stderr, "\nWebcam initialization failed on unsupported platform.\n");
240   (void)fflush(stderr);
241 #endif
242 }






References error_code, ERROR_WEBCAM,
    ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE, ERROR_WEBCAM_PERMISSION, and
    safe_fprintf().


Referenced by client_main().






image_t * webcam_read (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>


Capture a frame from global webcam.


Returns


Pointer to captured image, or NULL on error


Captures a single video frame from the global webcam interface.
    Returns an image_t structure containing the frame data in RGB format.
    The image is automatically converted from native webcam format to RGB.




Note


Returns NULL on error (device disconnected, I/O error,
  etc.).



Image structure is allocated internally and must NOT be freed by
    caller.




Subsequent calls reuse the same buffer (frame overwrites previous
    frame).




Frame rate is limited by webcam hardware and format
  negotiation.




For context-based operations, use webcam_read_context()
    instead.




Definition at line 65 of file
  video/webcam/webcam.c.


65                            {
66   // Check if test pattern mode is enabled
67   if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
68     // Allocate cached frame once (like FFmpeg's current_image)
69     // Reuse same buffer for each call to avoid repeated allocations
70     if (!cached_webcam_frame) {
71       cached_webcam_frame = image_new(320, 240);
72       if (!cached_webcam_frame) {
73         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate test pattern frame");
74         return NULL;
75       }
76     }
77 
78     // Generate animated test pattern each frame
79     // Animation is based on frame counter, respects FPS setting
80     unsigned int animation_phase = frame_counter / 2; // Slow down animation
81     frame_counter++;
82 
83     for (int y = 0; y < cached_webcam_frame->h; y++) {
84       for (int x = 0; x < cached_webcam_frame->w; x++) {
85         rgb_pixel_t *pixel = &cached_webcam_frame->pixels[y * cached_webcam_frame->w + x];
86 
87         // Animated color bars that shift based on frame counter
88         int animated_x = (x + animation_phase) % cached_webcam_frame->w;
89         int grid_x = animated_x / 40;
90 
91         // Base pattern: color bars that animate horizontally
92         switch (grid_x % 3) {
93         case 0: // Red
94           pixel->r = 255;
95           pixel->g = 0;
96           pixel->b = 0;
97           break;
98         case 1: // Green
99           pixel->r = 0;
100           pixel->g = 255;
101           pixel->b = 0;
102           break;
103         case 2: // Blue
104         default:
105           pixel->r = 0;
106           pixel->g = 0;
107           pixel->b = 255;
108           break;
109         }
110 
111         // Add animated grid lines
112         if (animated_x % 40 == 0 || y % 30 == 0) {
113           pixel->r = 0;
114           pixel->g = 0;
115           pixel->b = 0;
116         }
117       }
118     }
119 
120     return cached_webcam_frame;
121   }
122 
123   if (!global_webcam_ctx) {
124     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_WEBCAM, "Webcam not initialized - global_webcam_ctx is NULL");
125     return NULL;
126   }
127 
128   image_t *frame = webcam_read_context(global_webcam_ctx);
129 
130   if (!frame) {
131     return NULL;
132   }
133 
134   return frame;
135 }






References rgb_pixel_t::b, ERROR_MEMORY,
    ERROR_WEBCAM, frame, rgb_pixel_t::g, GET_OPTION,
    image_new(), rgb_pixel_t::r, SET_ERRNO, and
    webcam_read_context().






image_t * webcam_read_async (webcam_context_t *
  ctx)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>


Get the most recent frame from async camera thread
  (non-blocking).


Parameters


ctx Webcam context (must not be NULL)


Returns


Pointer to captured image, or NULL if no frame available
  yet


Retrieves the most recent frame from the background camera thread
    without blocking. Returns an image_t structure containing the latest
    frame data in RGB format, or NULL if the async thread hasn't yet captured a
    frame.




This function is used with async camera initialization to decouple
    slow camera I/O from the render loop. The background thread continuously
    captures frames at the camera's native frame rate, and this function
    retrieves the latest available frame on demand without waiting.




Note


Returns NULL if no frame has been captured yet by the
  background thread.



The caller takes ownership of the returned frame and must NOT free
    it.




Subsequent calls may return NULL if no new frame is available.




Only valid after webcam_init_context() has started the
    background thread.








image_t * webcam_read_context (webcam_context_t *
  ctx)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/webcam/webcam.h>


Capture a frame from webcam context.


Parameters


ctx Webcam context (must not be NULL)


Returns


Pointer to captured image, or NULL on error


Captures a single video frame from the specified webcam context.
    Returns an image_t structure containing the frame data in RGB format.
    The image is automatically converted from native webcam format to RGB.




Note


Returns NULL on error (device disconnected, I/O error,
  etc.).



Image structure is allocated internally and must NOT be freed by
    caller.




Subsequent calls reuse the same buffer (frame overwrites previous
    frame).




Frame rate is limited by webcam hardware and format
  negotiation.




Definition at line 263 of file
    video/webcam/webcam.c.


263                                                     {
264   (void)ctx;
265   SET_ERRNO(ERROR_WEBCAM, "Webcam read not supported on this platform");
266   return NULL;
267 }






References ERROR_WEBCAM, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by media_source_read_video(), and
    webcam_read().








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.12
    ascii-chat
